reasoning: The Lean formalization correctly captures: (1) twice-differentiability on (a,∞) via DifferentiableOn for both f and deriv f on Set.Ioi a; (2) the three least upper bounds via sSup of the respective absolute-value image sets; (3) the conclusion M₁² ≤ 4M₀M₂. The bddAbove hypotheses are necessary because Lean's sSup returns 0 (not +∞) for unbounded sets — so these are needed to make M₀, M₁, M₂ actually equal the "least upper bounds" described informally as real numbers. This is surfacing an implicit premise needed for the real-number encoding to match the informal LUBs. Moreover, hbdd1 is a consequence of the other two hypotheses plus the conclusion, so it doesn't narrow the scope. The formalization is a faithful reading.
